entry.title: Re: Migration Objects listed in Help Portal but not avail...
entry.content:
Hi @byd 

In the post, you have mentioned the App ID: F8638, so I assume you are using the app 'Explore Migration Objects'.
As stated in SAP Help Portal - Explore Migration Objects, "This app is only relevant for the migration approach Migrate Data Using Staging Tables. It does not include configuration elements, such as organizational units, or data that is not migrated, for example, employees managed in external systems.".

Could you please double check the help documentation and test again?

Best regards,
Irene

entry.title: CxO Payment Approval UX in My Inbox for Payment Requisitions - China
entry.content:
Why CxO’s Experience in Payment Approvals matters in Cloud ERP

In many Cloud ERP implementations, payment approval workflows are designed to “work”—but not necessarily to delight. That distinction matters more than most teams realize, especially when the final approvers are C‑level executives.

During a recent SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition deployment in China, a critical usability gap surfaced in the payment requisition approval experience for CxOs. The issue wasn’t functionality. It was experience.

And experience, at the executive level, determines adoption, cycle time, and ultimately trust in the system.

This is exactly why the new SAP Influence idea
“CxO Payment Approval UX in My Inbox for Payment Requisitions - China”
(described in https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/372233/?section=sectionDetails ) is so critical.

The Executive Reality: Approvals Happen in Between Everything Else

For CxOs—CFOs, CEOs, COOs, and senior finance leaders—payment approvals are rarely a primary task. They happen:

  • Between meetings
  • On mobile phones or tablets
  • Under time pressure
  • With legal and governance responsibility

In this context, every extra click matters.

Yet in the standard “My Inbox for Payment Requisitions – China” app, executives are required to navigate across six separate tabs just to understand one approval:

  • General Information
  • Requested Items
  • Payment Strategy
  • Attachments
  • Approval Details
  • Administrative Data

Critical business context—purchase orders, costs, margins, and original documents—are fragmented across the interface. The result Cognitive overload at exactly the moment when clarity is essential.

Where the Current Experience Falls Short

From an executive’s perspective, several pain points quickly emerge:

  1. No Information at a Glance

There is no single screen that answers the core executive questions:

  • What am I approving?
  • Why does it cost this much?
  • Is the documentation complete?
  • Who has approved this already?

The need to jump between tabs breaks decision flow.

  1. Attachments Are Out of Sight

Invoices, contracts, and supporting documents—often legally required—sit behind a separate tab. Executives can’t even tell at a glance whether documents exist.

  1. Missing Business-Critical Fields

Key data such as:

  • Purchase Order reference
  • Material or service details
  • Unit price and total amount
  • Gross margin

are either hidden or unavailable, forcing blind approvals or unnecessary rejection loops.

  1. Approval Actions Are Easy to Miss

Approve and Reject buttons are not consistently visible, especially on mobile devices, adding friction to the simplest part of the task: making the decision.

Rethinking Payment Approval Through a CxO Lens

The proposed redesign flips the paradigm from transaction-first to decision-first.

The guiding principle is simple:

Give executives everything they need to decide—on one screen, on any device, in seconds.

A Single, Scrollable Approval Page

All relevant information—financial data, line items, attachments, and approval history—appears on one consolidated page. No tabs. No hunting.

Business Context Front and Center

Key fields like Purchase Order number, material description, unit price, total amount (tax inclusive), and gross margin are surfaced immediately, enabling informed decisions.

Inline Attachments

Original business documents are visible directly within the approval screen. Executives can verify compliance without leaving context.

Clear Workflow Visibility

Approval steps and previous approvers are displayed inline alongside the business data, reinforcing accountability and governance.

Mobile-First by Design

The layout adapts naturally to phones and tablets, allowing approvals to happen wherever executives are—without compromising clarity.

Why CxO UX Is Not Optional in Cloud ERP

Improving executive UX isn’t a “nice-to-have.” It’s strategic.

Faster Cash Flow

Approval delays directly impact supplier payments and working capital. Removing friction accelerates decision-making.

Higher Adoption

If executives dislike the system, they will bypass it. That behavior quickly cascades through the organization.

Stronger Governance

Executives are legally and fiduciary responsible for approvals. Clear, complete information protects both the individual and the organization.

Better Customer References

Satisfied executives become champions—inside the company and in the market. Frustrated ones become blockers.

A Bigger Lesson for ERP Design

This case highlights a broader truth about Cloud ERP:

The most important users are often the least frequent users.

Designing for CxOs forces teams to prioritize:

  • Clarity over completeness
  • Insight over data volume
  • Decisions over transactions

When an approval experience works for a CEO on a mobile phone, it will work for everyone else too.

Final Thoughts

Payment approval is where finance, compliance, and executive accountability intersect. If the experience fails at this point, the entire ERP transformation is at risk.

By reimagining the payment approval UX around CxO needs, organizations don’t just improve a screen—they unlock faster operations, stronger governance, and greater confidence in their Cloud ERP investment.

Because when executives say “This system works for me,” the transformation truly succeeds.

entry.title: Re: BIB Query IT0001 WS_4
entry.content:
Hi, 
The scenario described it is impossible to achieve it using BIB. BIB is intended to create employees at EC level and replicate them which implies there are several mandatory Infotypes. This are the mandatory ITs as per BIB integration replication guide:
Mandatory SAP ERP Infotypes:

  • IT0000

  • IT0001

  • IT0002

This implies not only you will have to replicate IT0001 but also IT0000. If just 3 Infotypes are going to be mapped a custom iflow or either export from EC and IDOC upload would be better and once employee replication is needed configure it to desire ITs as well as manadory ones. 
This URL contain that info
Regards,

entry.title: Suppress Standard Address in Adobe Invoice Form – S/4HANA PublicCloud
entry.content:
We are working on an invoice output requirement in SAP S/4HANA Cloud (Public Edition) where the business needs to display a different company address on the Adobe invoice form based on a specific condition (Profit Center B0011).

To achieve this, we have:

  • Created a custom field at the billing document header level
  • Implemented custom logic (BAdI) to populate the field based on Profit Center
  • Maintained Output Parameter Determination (OPD) to trigger a custom form template
  • Modified the Adobe form to display the required (new) address

With this approach, we are successfully able to populate and display the new address.

Issue:
The standard company code address is still being rendered by the system along with the new address, resulting in an overlap on the invoice output.

We have attempted to control visibility within the Adobe form; however, the standard address block still appears, likely due to standard binding from the data source.

Query:
Is there a recommended approach in S/4HANA Cloud to suppress or override the standard company address in the billing document Adobe form so that only the custom address is displayed?

Any guidance on handling or controlling the standard address node/subform would be helpful.

entry.title: Re: PTP replication in Employee central payroll
entry.content:
Hi Barath, 
For PTP to know the mapping you have to use the guide. Then in the guide you have the fields mapping as mentioned already in the discussion. This will include which field of EC would be mapped to what field on EC. 
Then at ECP configuration level there is a logic of how to map it. This is at SPRO path 
Personnel Management-->Integration Settings for SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll-->Configuration of Point-to-Point Replication-->For Point-to-Point Replication. This are the options for mapping:
- I Identical mapping
- S Mapping via SAP table
- C Mapping via customer table
- SI Mapping via SAP table or identical mapping
- CI Mapping via customer table or identical mapping
Mainly there are 2 tables one sap one custom to map values coming from EC to ECP or identical value. 
Then to increase PTP functionality, you have to go to extensibility part of the guide and there are several options depending the scenario. If it is an included field, infotype or not. From configuration extensibility to BADI one. 
If you were able to implement BIB, PTP I would say it is simpler and also you have this report to check the replication RP_HRSFEC_REPLICATION_STATUS for PTP master data. 
Regards,

entry.title: Re: Run Datasphere Task Chains from SAC Multi-Actions via External API Step
entry.content:
Hi @ambarish_kmd06580

 A 404 error usually indicates system can not find the task chain or task chain log you've specified or the tech user defined in oauth client does not has the privilege. It seems like a configuration issue. Could you please try sending below requests to ensure your task chain public API works well in DSP with the oauth client you specified? You may send 3 requests in sequence:

1. Get the access token of the oAuth client you specified in DSP, it should return the access token you need to use in next requests.
curl --request POST \
  --url https://<your dsp token url> \
  --header 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' \
  --data grant_type=client_credentials \
  --data 'client_id=<your oauth client id>' \
  --data 'client_secret=<your oauth client secret>'

 

2. Run the task chain and it should return {logId: xxxxxx}
curl --request POST \
  --url https://<your dsp host>/api/v1/datasphere/tasks/chains/<your DSP space>/run/<task chain id> \
  --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  --header 'Authorization: Bearer <the access token your get from the response of first step>'

 

3. Run the query to get the status of the task chain run
curl --request GET \
  --url https://<your dsp host>/api/v1/datasphere/tasks/logs/<your DSP space>/<the log id you get from previous step> \
  --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  --header 'Authorization: Bearer <the access token your get from the response of first step>'

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: G/L does not exist in company Code during Automatic P...
entry.content:
Hi

 

The account determination replaces the account symbol BANK with the G/L account number of the house bank account defined in the house bank master data. The appropriate masking can also be used for the bank subaccounts. in your scenario below will be masking 

  1. Main Bank Account- ++++++++++ 
  2. Incoming G/L Account-+++++1++++
  3. Outgoing G/L Account- +++++2++++

+ replaced by GL account in House Bank Account 

Check in FBZP House Bank details.  Your GL should be 10200012

entry.title: SAP Business Network for Logistics 2604 – What’s New?
entry.content:
We’re excited to share the latest innovations across SAP Business Network Freight Collaboration and SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace delivered with the April 2026 release. This update focuses on enabling scalable carrier collaboration, improving operational control at docks and gates, and extending flexibility in modelling and visualizing tracking data.

Highlights at a glance:

 In addition to our functional updates, we are happy to share two new customer success stories with you in this edition, so make sure to read to the end.

 

Cross SAP Business Network Freight Collaboration and SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace

Self‑service testing enabled for carriers and data contributors

Carriers and data contributors can now use the Test Service functionality in test tenants to independently establish a connection with a test shipper and perform self‑service testing of their outbound integrations.

An internal shipper tenant is used to trigger the payload, with the shipper LBN ID and order ID predefined by SAP Business Network for Logistics. Two testing options are available:

  • With Default Payload – a static payload designed to validate connectivity from SAP Business Network for Logistics to the carrier system
  • With Custom Payload – allows carriers to modify order details to test different business scenarios

This enhancement reduces dependency on shipper coordination and accelerates onboarding and testing for carriers and data contributors registered after May 2024.

Scaling carrier collaboration: New partner service from Coneksion

To further support onboarding at scale, a new partner service from Coneksion is now available. The service leverages pre‑boarded carrier connection layers, simplifying connectivity and reducing integration effort when collaborating with large carrier networks.

 

SAP Business Network Freight Collaboration

Freight contracting EDI supports standalone products

Freight contracting EDI has been enhanced to support standalone products. With this release IFTMIN (UN/EDIFACT) can now map product item descriptions to FTX segments

The EDI documentation has been updated accordingly

Dock appointment scheduling: Granular role‑based access control

New, more granular roles are now available for Dock Appointment Scheduling and Gate Operations:

  • ManageDockAppointmentsDisplayTemplate
  • ManageDockAppointmentsProcessTemplate
  • ManageGateOperationsDisplayTemplate
  • ManageGateOperationsProcessTemplate

These roles are offered as an alternative to the existing roles and allow customers to restrict access exclusively to the ‘Manage Dock Appointments’ and ‘Manage Gate Operations’ apps. While existing roles remain fully supported, the new roles help enforce the principle of least privilege by limiting editing and navigation rights more precisely.

Dock Appointment Scheduling: Check-In on Hold now processed as an event

To improve execution transparency, Check‑In on Hold is now processed as a distinct event in the ‘Manage Freight Execution’ app.

Dock appointment scheduling: New fields for truck and trailer ID

To reduce manual entry and improve data consistency, users can now separately capture:

  • Truck ID
  • Trailer ID
  • License plate and country/region of registration

When truck and trailer IDs are entered in the ‘Freight Orders for Confirmation’ app, the data is synchronized with ‘Manage Dock Appointments’ and ‘Manage Gate Operations’, provided that no changes have already been made.

The new fields are available in the ‘Resource Details’ section, and the license plate field has been moved from ‘Driver Details’ to ‘Resource Details’.

In addition, the Consumer API for Dock Appointment Scheduling (GenericAppointment_Out) and the Consumer API to Manage Gate Operations (GenericGateProcess_Out) have been enhanced accordingly.

 

SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace

Visibility control now also available for standard model fields

In the ‘Manage Models’ app, customers can now control the visibility of standard model fields, in addition to custom fields.

If the Visible checkbox is not selected, the corresponding standard model field is hidden from:

  • Filters and worklist settings
  • The Information tab of detail pages in fulfillment tracking apps

This enables cleaner, role‑specific UIs aligned with individual business requirements.

User‑defined tables can be added to tracking unit detail pages

Modelling flexibility has been further enhanced. Customers can now define Composition and Association‑to‑Many field types in the ‘Manage Models’ app and display them as user‑defined tables in the tracking unit detail page.

 

Previously, only plain user‑defined fields could be displayed. With this enhancement, more complex and structured datasets can be visualized directly in the tracking UI.

Improved data consistency with SAP TM in SAP S/4HANA

Data synchronization between SAP Transportation Management in SAP S/4HANA and Global Track and Trace has been refined. Only the last correlated planned event is now synchronized, preventing inconsistencies and ensuring cleaner event timelines.

Shipment applications become more user‑friendly

Several usability enhancements have been introduced:

  • Standard Point Location Code (SPLC) is now displayed in location popovers across all fulfillment tracking apps
  • In the ‘Track Shipments’ app, search is now supported based on Carrier Reference Document ID

These enhancements make it easier to identify shipments and locations, especially in high‑volume scenarios.

 

Celebrating our customers’ success

On top of this month’s release highlights, we are proud to highlight two customer success stories that demonstrate the value of SAP Business Network for Logistics across different industries and transformation journeys.

Sasol, a global integrated energy and chemicals company, was recognized last week as a 2026 Innovation Award Winner for reimagining its logistics operations through intelligent transport optimization and real‑time freight collaboration. By implementing SAP S/4HANA Transportation Management together with SAP Business Network for Logistics, Sasol transformed a largely 3PL‑operated model into an in‑house, digitally connected transportation capability. This approach enabled greater flexibility, improved transportation planning and scheduling, and tighter alignment with Sasol’s business strategy focused on service performance and customer satisfaction.

View their pitch deck for more details.

In addition, we are excited to share a newly agreed reference story with Salling Group A/S, one of Europe’s largest retailers. Salling Group set out to replace manual, email‑driven coordination and fragmented logistics processes with an integrated, data‑driven approach. By leveraging SAP Business Network Freight Collaboration and SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace, the retailer gained clear, connected, and comprehensive visibility across its supply chain—from purchase to store—while simplifying collaboration with carriers and improving control over execution.

Go to their case study on sap.com

These customer stories highlight how SAP Business Network for Logistics helps organizations across industries increase transparency, strengthen collaboration, and build more resilient supply chains.

 

------------------------------------------------------

The above summarizes the key innovations for 2604. We hope you enjoy the new capabilities. Thanks for reading the blog post and stay tuned! 

A full overview of the 2604 release can be found under the following link.

To learn more about SAP Business Network for Logistics visit SAP - Business Network for Logistics.

For further questions and feedback related to the blog, please check the Q&A area and feel free to post your questions in the link with the tag SAP Business Network for Logistics.

entry.title: Ask Me Anything with SAP Champions – Monthly Series (Session 3)
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community and #ABAPlovers

My name is Sandra Solís, an ABAP Developer with a focus on ABAP, ABAP Cloud, and modern SAP development practices. I’m passionate about building clean, efficient solutions and continuously learning new technologies within the SAP ecosystem.

From my experience in SAP projects, learning sessions, and community events, the most valuable moments are always the same: Asking questions — any kind of question — and getting honest, practical answers from people who have been there.

For this reason, I’m launching the session 3 of AMA (Ask Me Anything) series.

This space is dedicated to all things ABAP:
classic ABAP, ABAP Cloud, RAP, performance tuning, clean code, debugging, real project challenges, and even career growth in SAP development.

You are invited to ask any questions you have, openly or anonymously. No question is too simple or too complex. Let’s make this a space for sharing real experiences, solving doubts, and learning together as a community.

You can drop your question in the comments below.

or you can ask anonymously here:  Ask Me Anything Champion - Session 3 ABAP

Submit your questions during this month, and selected ones will be answered in a follow-up  video recap. Looking forward to your questions 🚀

entry.title: Re: Automatic Payment - DMEE File
entry.content:
Hi

When transferring data to a bank whether through a direct integration or a manual portal upload it is important to distinguish between two key dates:

File Header (Submission Date): This is the date the file or data packet is shared with the bank. It represents when the bank receives the instruction.

Line Item (Value Date): This is the specific date assigned to an individual payment record. The Value Date tells the bank exactly when to execute the transfer.

How it works: Banks can receive and store these instructions ahead of time. If the Value Date is in the future, the bank will hold the payment in a "pending" or "queued" status and only process the settlement once that specific date is reached.

entry.title: Re: 1H 2026 Release: Streamlining Data after Employment Terminations in SuccessFactors Employee Cent
entry.content:
Hi @almamontejano10,

The new setting is available for all event reasons, both existing and newly created ones.

It is delivered in a downward‑compatible way, meaning the default value of the setting is disabled. Only when this option is explicitly enabled within an event reason configuration, records will be automatically be deleted when they have that event reason and when the start date of that record is after the Termination Date. The deletion runs as part of the terminate process.
This ensures full control and no impact on existing customer configurations unless the setting is intentionally activated.

entry.title: Customer Engagement (IS‑U): “Transaction” menu greyed out for Installment Plans
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am working as an SAP Consultant on Customer Engagement (CE) with SAP IS‑U.

In Customer Engagement, we have Installment Plans available in the left‑hand navigation panel. When expanding this menu, we can see options such as:

  • Show Installment Plans
  • Edit Installment Plan
  • Installment Plan History

When we select one of these options, the system correctly navigates to the Installment Plan view, where we can see:

  • Installment plan details
  • Master data
  • Installment plan history

The Business Partner is correctly confirmed, and the installment plan data is displayed without issues.

However, in the top‑right area of the screen, there is a “Transaction” button/menu. This menu contains several actions, such as:

  • Show Installment Plan
  • Edit Installment Plan
  • Show Payment plan/list
  • Edit Payment plan/list
  • Show Installment Plan Schedule
  • Edit Installment Plan Schedule

All of these transaction options are greyed out and cannot be selected.

There are no error messages shown. The installment plan can be displayed, but none of the transactions in the “Transaction” menu can be executed.

What is the root cause for the “Transaction” menu being greyed out in the Installment Plan view in Customer Engagement with SAP IS‑U?

Specifically:

  • Which customizing settings or parameters are typically missing or not correctly set?
  • Is this related to Transaction Launcher configuration, logical system / WebGUI setup, business role or navigation profile assignment, or installment plan status / locks?
  • Which SPRO customizing paths should be checked to enable these transaction options?

Any guidance on the correct setup or common pitfalls for this scenario would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your support.

entry.title: SAP Fiori Elements List Report: Implementing Dual Data Views with Flat and Hierarchical Entities
entry.content:
In many SAP Fiori Elements applications, data is modeled hierarchically. Consider a scenario with a 3-level entity structure:

  • Header (parent)
  • Item (child)
  • SubItem (grandchild)

By default, the List Report displays data at the Header level. However, there is a requirement to provide a Detailed view: a flat representation that surfaces fields from all three levels in a single row, without requiring navigation into each record.

Goal:

Segmented button on the List Report that lets the user toggle between:

  • Detailed View: flat structure, fields from all 3 levels in one row
  • Normal View: standard hierarchical view at the Header level

Why this approach

Instead of modifying the existing hierarchical entity, a separate flat view entity allows both representations to coexist without impacting the original data model.

 

Backend: Exposing a Flat View Entity (CAP)

The standard Header entity only holds parent-level fields. To surface child and grandchild fields in a single flat list, a dedicated view entity is exposed in the CAP service.

This entity (HeaderDetailView) is built using a bottom-to-top approach, starting from SubItem and joining upward through Item to Header, making all fields available at a single level.

// In schema.cds

entity HeaderDetailView as select from SubItem {
    // SubItem fields
    subItemField1 as field1,
    subItemField2 as field2,

    // Item fields (via association)
    item.itemField1 as field3
    item.itemField2 as field4

    // Header fields (via association)
    item.header.headerField1 as field5,
    item.header.headerField2 as field6,
    item.header.ID as ID
};

This entity is then exposed in the service:

// In service.cds

service AppService {
    entity Header         as projection on db.Header;
    entity HeaderDetailView as projection on db.HeaderDetailView;
}

 

UI: Wiring Two Entities into One List Report

The UI layer uses annotations and manifest configuration to expose both entities as separate views within the same List Report.

Step 1: Define LineItem annotations for both views

Detailed view: on HeaderDetailView:

//In Annotations.cds file

annotate service.HeaderDetailView with @(UI: { 
    HeaderInfo                       : {
        TypeName      : 'Item',
        TypeNamePlural: 'Items'
    },
    PresentationVarinat #DetailedView: {
        Visualizations: [{$AnnotationPath:'HeaderDetailView/@UI.LineItem#DetailedView'}],
        GroupBy       : [ID],
    },
    PresentationVarinat #NormalView  : {
        Visualizations: [{$AnnotationPath:'Header/@UI.LineItem'}],
    },
    LineItem #DetailedView           : [
        {
            $Type            : 'UI.DataField',
            Value            : ID,
            ![@UI.Importance]: #High
        },
        {
            $Type            : 'UI.DataField',
            Value            : field1,
            ![@UI.Importance]: #High
        },
        //add other fields in similar fashion
    ],
});

Normal view: on Header:

//In Annotations.cds file

annotate service.Header with @(UI: { 
    HeaderInfo                       : {
        TypeName      : 'Item',
        TypeNamePlural: 'Items'
    },
    LineItem          : [
        {
            $Type            : 'UI.DataField',
            Value            : ID,
            ![@UI.Importance]: #High
        },
        {
            $Type            : 'UI.DataField',
            Value            : field1,
            ![@UI.Importance]: #High
        },
        //add other header level-fields in similar fashion
    ],
});

 

Step 2: Define SelectionPresentationVariant on both entities

This is what the List Report uses to derive the tab label and resolve the correct LineItem.

//In Annotations.cds file

annotate service.HeaderDetailView with @(UI: {
    SelectionPresentationVariant #DetailedView: {
        $Type              : 'UI.SelectionPresentationVariantType',
        Text               : 'Detailed View',
        PresentationVariant: {
            Visualizations: ['@UI.LineItem#DetailedView']
        }
    }
});

annotate service.Header with @(UI: { 
    SelectionPresentationVariant #NormalView: {
        $Type              : 'UI.SelectionPresentationVariantType',
        Text               : 'Normal View',
        PresentationVariant: {
            Visualizations: ['@UI.LineItem']
        }
    }
});

 

Step 3: Configure manifest.json

"targets": {
        "HeaderList": {
          "type": "Component",
          "id": "HeaderList",
          "name": "sap.fe.templates.ListReport",
          "options": {
            "settings": {
              "contextPath": "/HeaderDetailView",
              "views": {
                "paths": [
                  {
                    "key": "tab1",
                    "annotationPath": "com.sap.vocabularies.UI.v1.SelectionPresentationVariant#DetailedView"
                  },
                  {
                    "key": "tab2",
                    "entitySet": "ObjectPageEntity",
                    "annotationPath": "com.sap.vocabularies.UI.v1.SelectionPresentationVariant#NormalView"
                  }
                ]

              },
              
              "enhanceI18n": "i18n/i18n.properties",
              "variantManagement": "Page",
              "initialLoad": "Enabled",
              "navigation": {
                "ObjectPageEntity": {
                  "detail": {
                    "route": "ObjectPageEntityRoute"
                  }
                }
              },
              "controlConfiguration": {
                "@com.sap.vocabularies.UI.v1.SelectionPresentationVariant#DetailedView": {
                  "tableSettings": {
                    "type": "ResponsiveTable",
                    "disableCopyToClipboard": true,
                    "enablePaste": false,
                    "personalization": {
                      "column": true,
                      "sort": true,
                      "filter": true,
                      "group": true
                    }
                  },
                  "columns": {
                  }
                },
                "/Header/@com.sap.vocabularies.UI.v1.SelectionPresentationVariant#NormalView": {
                  "tableSettings": {
                    "type": "ResponsiveTable",
                    "disableCopyToClipboard": true,
                    "enablePaste": false,
                    "personalization": {
                      "filter": true,
                      "group":false
                    }
                  },
                  "columns": {
                 }
           }
        },

This configuration enables the List Report to render multiple views backed by different entity sets.

Key points:

  • contextPath points to the flat view entity (HeaderDetailView) as the primary entity
  • Tab2 uses entitySet to point to the hierarchical entity (Header)
  • annotationPath must point to SelectionPresentationVariant (not LineItem), as it provides both the visualization reference and the tab label.

Result

The List Report renders a segmented button on the table toolbar, allowing users to switch between Detailed View and Normal View. Thus, both views are available at the same List Report level, without requiring navigation.

>> Normal View:

>> Detailed View:

Summary

Comparison of Views:

 

Detailed View

Normal View

Entity

HeaderDetailView (flat)

Header (hierarchical)

Structure

Fields from all 3 levels

Parent-level fields only

Annotation

UI.LineItem#DetailedView

UI.LineItem

Variant

SelectionPresentationVariant#DetailedView

SelectionPresentationVariant#NormalView

 

Final takeaway

SAP Fiori Elements List Report supports multiple views backed by different entity sets, not just filtered variants of the same entity. SelectionPresentationVariant acts as the bridge between CDS annotations and manifest configuration, controlling both the tab label and the rendered content.

When to use this approach

  • When a hierarchical model needs a flattened analytical view
  • When users need detailed data without navigating to Object Pages
  • When multiple representations of the same data are required in a single List Report

entry.title: Preparation to Migration of New Accrual Engine
entry.content:
Starting with release S/4HANA 1809, the new S/4HANA Accrual Engine becomes available. The programs for the Manual Accruals application have transitioned from the classic Accrual Engine to the S/4HANA Accrual Engine. As a result, you need to migrate the data and customizing settings for Manual Accruals from the old Accrual Engine to the S/4HANA Accrual Engine after upgrading to release S/4HANA 1809. If you've been using Manual Accruals before the upgrade and want to continue using them afterward, this migration is mandatory.  (SAP KBA 3701921)

The engine enables complex postings. You can define posting schemas that allow more than two line items. It also allows optional review and/or approval of periodic accruals at the period end. 

Migration Activities (ACEMIGIMG) 

The data model of customizing and transactional data of the (new) S/4HANA Accrual Engine has changed compared to the old Accrual Engine. Several of the old tables have been replaced by new tables. As a consequence, both types of tables, customizing and transactional data, need to be migrated after the upgrade to S/4HANA 1809 or higher. 

This migration is necessary after an upgrade from: 

  • A ‘classic’ ERP release 
  • An S/4HANA Finance (aka sFIN) release 
  • An S/4HANA release < 1809 

Note: The transactional data of the Accrual Engine consists of: 

  • Accrual Objects 
  • Accrual Postings 

Required migration of transparent tables: 

 

 

 

 

Timing and Independence of Migration 

The migration of Accrual Engine transactional data can be performed after the migration of all other postings in the system (either of the migration tool from ECC to S/4HANA). This process is technically independent from the migration of other postings, such as FI or CO postings. The advantages of this separate migration include: 

  • System Usability: The system can be used, and all kinds of postings can be performed even if the Accrual Engine has not yet been migrated. However, applications that rely on the Accrual Engine will not function until the migration is complete. 

 

  • Flexible Timing: The migration of the Accrual Engine can be scheduled at a different time, such as on a separate weekend. This is particularly useful if the initial migration activities are time-consuming and adding the Accrual Engine migration would exceed the available timeframe. 

 

  • Separate Tool: Technically, the migration of Accrual Engine transactional data uses a separate tool compared to the migration of other postings. It employs the mass data processing cockpit, which is an SAP standard tool for handling mass data processing. 

 

Migration of Accrual Engine Data for Customers Upgrading to S/4HANA 1809 

Customers upgrading from S/4HANA releases earlier than 1809 or from S/4HANA Finance to release 1809 are required to migrate the Accrual Engine data for Manual Accruals. Although these customers have already completed the conversion to S/4HANA, they may not typically revisit the IMG folder labeled "Conversion of Accounting to SAP S/4HANA." 

Error Message Notification 

To ensure that such customers are aware of the need to migrate the Accrual Engine, an error message will be triggered by the system whenever the old Accrual Engine is used for Manual Accruals (ACE_S4001).

entry.title: Planning with mode coupling(mode linkage) in discrte industry
entry.content:
Dear ePP/DS Gurus,

My business scenario for planning an scheduling is as follows.

Material X has 3 operations(Activitity P), for example 10, 20, 30, and each operation has alternative modes in PDS.

Operation(Activity P) : 10, Alternative Modes : AA1(Mode No 1), AA2(Mode No 2), AA3(Mode No 3)
Operation(Activity P) : 20, Alternative Modes : BB1(Mode No 1), BB2(Mode No 2)
Operation(Activity P) : 30, Alternative Modes : CC1(Mode No 1), CC2(Mode No 2), CC3(Mode No 3)
(Mode Linkage Type : Resource-Independent)

When it comes to scheduling, the relationships between Activities in my scenario are as follows.

AA1 or AA2 -> BB1 -> CC1 or CC2
AA3               -> BB2 -> CC3

I tried to use Resource Network for modeling the relationships but an error message, "Order could  ot be scheduled", is raised after executing SAP_PP_001 in Product View, which is the same symptoms in Note 3496388.

So I changed Mode No for each Mode with BAdi as follows.
Mode      Mode No
AA1          1
AA2          1
AA3          2
BB1          1
BB2          2
CC1          1
CC2          1
CC3          2

After changing Mode No, still left Material assigned with Resource Network, I executed Heuristic again. In this time, another error message is raised saying "Attempt was made to create two modes with the same number for a segment"

And found Note 1625010, 1625007, but these are not related with my scenario which is about MRP-based Detailed Schedulig.

Please guide me to reslove the problem and let me know how to configure for my scenario.

 

Thanks in advance for your cooperation. 

Best Regards,
Sean Lee

entry.title: Re: SAP UI5: ISSUE : Calling Deep Entity of JSON - SAP UI...
EWM Pack Specification: How to find the table/link between internal node GUID and Pack Spec ID?
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I am working with EWM Packaging Specifications in transaction /SCWM/PACKSPEC and I am trying to understand how the internal technical identifier stored in the below tables is related to the displayed Pack Spec ID on the screen of /SCWM/PACKSPEC.

Tables:

Header- /SCWM/S_PS_DL_HEADER
Content - /SCWM/S_PS_DL_CONTENT
Level - /SCWM/S_PS_DL_LEVEL
Level Element - /SCWM/S_PS_DL_LEVEL_ELEMENT

Basically, it is getting difficult to identify the Pack Specification created in /SCWM/PACKSPEC. When you open the tables to see the data, the Pack Spec ID is not stored in the tables, instead a GUID (Internal Node Number) is stored. So wanted to find a way which can give me a relation between them.

What I found so far:

  • The field PS_ID is shown in structure /SCWM/S_PS_HEADER_INT
  • Data element is /SCWM/DE_PSID
  • So this looks like a structure field, not a direct database table field

My doubt is:

Is there any standard SAP table that gives the relation between:

  • the internal node/header GUID (technical number, for example from pack spec tables), and
  • the normal Pack Spec ID (PS_ID) shown in /SCWM/PACKSPEC?

I am specifically looking for a table or standard object that can help convert/map:
internal GUID / node number → Pack Spec ID

If anyone knows:

  • the exact table name,
  • a CDS/view,
  • or the standard relationship between these objects,

that would really help.

 

Thank You

entry.title: Re: Joule Studio: Why is build_default required if standard plan exists?
entry.content: BTP entitlements are additive at the subaccount level. Assigning “build_default” does not remove or downgrade “standard,” and it will not affect running workflows, RPA bots, monitoring, or other capabilities already enabled by the “standard” plan. Best practice is to keep your “standard” entitlement for productive use and, if the activation requires it, add “build_default” to satisfy the Joule Studio checks.

entry.title: Re: Custom Validation not working for create incoming inv...
entry.content:
Dear @K12,

I reproduced your test cases in my own environment and below are my conclusions together with example code snippets and screenshots:

For Create Supplier Invoice app the correct enhancement spot is MRM_CHECK_INVOICE_CLOUD
As a simple trigger I used field headerdata-UNPLANNEDDELIVERYCOST and checked whether it is initial. In my test the custom message was triggered correctly once the document context was complete

IF headerdata-UNPLANNEDDELIVERYCOST IS INITIAL.

  APPEND VALUE #(

    messagetype      = if_ex_mrm_check_invoice_cloud=>c_messagetype_warning

    messageid        = 'MRM_BADI_CLOUD'

    messagenumber    = '001'

    messagevariable1 = 'TEST'

  ) TO messages.

ENDIF.

For Create Supplier Invoice Advanced the same enhancement spot applies MRM_CHECK_INVOICE_CLOUD BUT only for error NOT for warning based on information below, last text starting from Please note that….

I confirmed that also in my tenant

IF headerdata-UNPLANNEDDELIVERYCOST IS INITIAL.

  APPEND VALUE #(

    messagetype      = if_ex_mrm_check_invoice_cloud=>c_messagetype_error

    messageid        = 'MRM_BADI_CLOUD'

    messagenumber    = '001'

    messagevariable1 = 'TEST'

  ) TO messages.

ENDIF.

For Create Incoming Invoices the correct extension layer is different. SAP describes as the app used to create supplier invoices or credit memos without purchase order reference in Financial Accounting. Because of that the correct Badi is FIN_ACDOC_VALIDATION, not MRM_CHECK_INVOICE_CLOUD

As a simple technical trigger I used header field accountingdocheader-AccountingDocumentHeaderText and checked whether it is initial. This worked on Post. SAP also explicitly documents that FIN_ACDOC_VALIDATION supports both Error and Warning messages and recommends standard journal entry validation rules for simpler header or item checks

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/1cbcff7ccd35405ab445b223c1ab1588/fed79813a60447669f096d9fb0713e16.html

IF accountingdocheader-AccountingDocumentHeaderText IS INITIAL.

  validationmessage-msgid = 'FIN_SUB_VAL'.

  validationmessage-msgty = 'W'.

  validationmessage-msgno = '000'.

  validationmessage-msgv1 = ‘TEST’.

  RETURN.

ENDIF.

 

 

Summary:

  • Create Supplier Invoice use MRM_CHECK_INVOICE_CLOUD; warning worked
  • Create Supplier Invoice Advanced use MRM_CHECK_INVOICE_CLOUD; error worked
  • Create Incoming Invoices (FB60) use FIN_ACDOC_VALIDATION; warning worked

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Navigating the depths of Data Aging in S/4 HANA
entry.content:
DimensionData AgingNSE (Native Storage Extension)Data Archiving / ILM
What it doesMoves data to cold HANA partition (still in DB, disk-resident)Offloads columns/tables to disk within HANA, transparent to applicationRemoves data from DB entirely; stores in archive files or ILM store
Data still in DB?YesYesNo
Memory reductionYes — cold partition not loaded into RAMYes — offloaded pages not in main memoryYes — data removed entirely
Disk space reductionNoNoYes
Backup size reductionNoNoYes
Custom code impactHigh — Z-programs querying aged tables silently return incomplete data unless temperature context is explicitly setNone — fully transparentMedium — depends on archiving info structures and access paths
Application coverageLimited — only SAP-delivered Data Aging Objects; custom objects require framework developmentAny table, including Z-tablesBroad — SAP ArchiveObjects for most application areas; ILM for retention-managed data
Regulatory / DPP complianceNo — data physically remains in DBNo — data physically remains in DBYes — supports blocking, deletion, and destruction under GDPR/local law
Reversible?Yes, but requires re-partitioningYes, easilyNo — retrieval is possible but not a rollback
Operational overheadHigh — partition management per landscape, non-transportable, aging runs to schedule/monitorLow — set and forget at column or table levelMedium — archive runs, retrieval config, ILM policy setup
Best forShort-term memory relief on application data with no immediate archiving pathTechnical/logging tables, large audit tables, Z-tables with historical dataPrimary long-term strategy for all application data; mandatory for DPP scenarios
RecommendationUse sparingly, as a tactical bridge — never as a permanent strategyPreferred over Data Aging for technical data; low risk, low effortDefault recommendation for all application data lifecycle management

entry.title: Re: ORA-01792 (Database error 1792 at EXEC) in SAP ECC on...
entry.content:
Hi,

Lets start with the fact that Oracle 12.1 was supported for only a very-very short time and it is no longer supported for SAP Systems since July 2022. Following SAPs recommendation about the supported versions, one always have to install the latest Bundle Patch on a supported Oracle database release as soon as possible. Please check below SAP notes:
1174136 - Oracle: End of Support Dates

Considering these information, some of your question is no longer valid.

For "point 1" I don't really have a answer, but sure thing is that we have an SAP note that deals exactly with this error:
2413016 - ORA-1792 creating view CDS_DS_CCS

Do not ignore this error as it will later lead to further errors as soon as the related and dependent objects will be accessed by business processes. 
There are no risk to install the latest Bundle Patch if all is well prepared. Please test the Bundle Patch installation first on an identical system of your productive to avoid any issues later on with the real PROD system.

entry.title: Prerequisites for Creating Accrual Objects and Performing Accrual Postings
entry.content:
Before accrual objects can be created and accrual postings can be performed, specific Customizing settings must be maintained. These settings are used to define the following:

  • The types of accruals to be calculated and posted
  • The algorithms by which accruals will be calculated
  • The accrual accounts to be used during posting

The required Customizing activities can be accessed through the Accruals Management section of the Implementation Guide (IMG), either via:

  • Transaction ACEIMG, where the Accruals Management IMG tree is displayed directly
  • Transaction SPRO, by navigating through the IMG to the relevant node

 

The Customizing settings for Purchase Order Accruals are part of a separate application. These settings do not need to be configured if only manual accruals are to be used.

The Customizing settings required for Manual Accruals consist of two main components:

1. Settings for the Accrual Engine
Since the Manual Accruals application is built on the Accrual Engine, these settings must be configured first. They also serve as a prerequisite for the Customizing settings used in Purchase Order Accruals.

Important: The Accrual Engine is designed as a generic tool. Therefore, not all available settings need to be configured if the Accrual Engine is intended to be used only for Purchase Order Accruals.

 

2. Settings specific to the Manual Accruals application
These settings are used to define parameters and rules that apply only to the Manual Accruals functionality

entry.title: Efficient ABAP Query Design for Cleared Vendor and Customer Items (BKPF–BSAK / BKPF–BSAD)
entry.content:
Requirement Statement
We need to design efficient SELECT queries involving the following SAP tables:
  • BKPF (Accounting Document Header)
  • BSAK (Vendor Cleared Items)
  • BSAD (Customer Cleared Items)
Selection Screen Inputs
  • Mandatory fields:
    • Company Code (BUKRS)
    • Account Type (KOART)
    • Clearing Date (AUGDT)
    • Document Type (BLART)
    • Posting Key (BSCHL)
  • Optional fields:
    • Customer/Vendor Number (KUNNR / LIFNR)
    • Document Number (BELNR)
Challenge
  • In BKPF, the primary key requires BUKRS, BELNR, and GJAHR.
  • However, in the program:
    • Fiscal Year (GJAHR) is not available as input.
    • Document Number (BELNR) is optional.
We must therefore design queries that efficiently retrieve cleared items from BKPF + BSAK and BKPF + BSAD, while handling the absence of fiscal year input and optional document number.

entry.title: Re: SAP SD Consultant role (OTC, S/4HANA)
entry.content:
You have not indicated as to how many years of SAP SD experience you have based on which, the advice can differ. 

You can simulate mini-projects in a sandbox system to build confidence and it would also be a practical hands-on scenarios to practice

1) Pricing Procedure Setup
Create a new pricing procedure for a specific sales org. Include discounts, surcharges, freight, and taxes. Test it end-to-end with a sales order and billing.

2) Order-to-Cash Flow
Configure a new sales document type (e.g., ZOR). Link it to delivery and billing types. Run through the full cycle and check FI postings.

3) Third-Party Order Scenario
Configure item category for third-party processing. Test integration with MM (purchase requisition creation).

4) Credit Management
Set up credit control area and risk categories. Test blocking/unblocking of sales orders.

5) Output Determination
Configure invoice outputs (PDF/email). Test condition records for different customers.

entry.title: Re: mb5b and MB5w data miss match in TSP
entry.content:
Why MB5B and MB5W differ is because

1) Report Purpose

  • MB5B (Stock on Posting Date) - Shows stock values based on material documents and valuation at a specific posting date. It’s more of a financial view tied to accounting.
  • MB5W (Stock Valuation) - Shows stock values based on current inventory quantities and valuation class. It’s more of a logistics view tied to material management.

2) Valuation Method

  • MB5B uses actual postings (GR/IR, FI documents).
  • MB5W uses valuation class and moving average price or standard price. If prices changed after the posting date, MB5W will reflect the latest valuation, not the historical one.

3) Data Source

  • MB5B pulls from accounting tables (BKPF, BSEG). In S/4HANA, it would be ACDOCA 
  • MB5W pulls from material management tables (MBEW, MKPF, MSEG). Different sources → different logic.  In S/4HANA, it would be MATDOC 

Below would be the practical investigation approach

  • Run MB5B and MB5W for the same key date and same company code/materials.
  • Check valuation method (standard vs moving average price).
  • Compare FI postings vs MM stock quantities for a few materials.
  • Look for open GR/IR balances — MB5B will show them, MB5W may not.
  • Check if MB5W is run with “as of date” — if not, it shows current values, not historical.

Having said that, my advice would be

  • Use MB5B when reconciling with FI (financial reporting).
  • Use MB5W when reconciling with MM (inventory management).
  • For reconciliation projects, many companies use MB5L (List of Stock Values), which is designed to bridge FI and MM differences.

entry.title: OData V4 Fiori Elements: "KeyPath missing at p.getItemFromContext" when selecting Value Help on a CD
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts,

I am facing a persistent UI5 crash in an OData V4 Fiori Elements application (based on ABAP RAP).

The Scenario: I have a Root Consumption View with an input Parameter. I have assigned a custom CDS Value Help (Dropdown / #XS) to this parameter. The dropdown successfully opens and displays the data in the Fiori Preview.

The Error: When the user clicks/selects an item from the dropdown list, the slection doesn't happen with the following console error in the browser:

Uncaught (in promise) Error: KeyPath missing at p.getItemFromContext

1. Value Help CDS View (Single Key, Dropdown)

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Value Help for Parameter'
@ObjectModel.resultSet.sizeCategory: #XS  

define view entity ZI_VH_CustomValue
  as select from custom_domain_table
{
      @UI.lineItem: [{ position: 10 }]
        key cast( domain_value as abap.char(1) ) as ValueKey,

      @UI.lineItem: [{ position: 20 }]
      @Semantics.text: true
      domain_text as Description
}
where active_status = 'A'

2. The Consumption View (Short Version)

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Main Business Object'
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true

define root view entity ZC_MainBusinessObject
  with parameters

    @EndUserText.label: 'Custom Filter Parameter'
    @Consumption.valueHelpDefinition: [{
        entity: { name: 'ZI_VH_CustomValue', element: 'ValueKey' },

        /* Explicit binding to pass the value back to the parameter */
        additionalBinding: [{
            localParameter: 'p_custom_filter',
            element: 'ValueKey',
            usage: #RESULT
        }]
    }]
    p_custom_filter : z_custom_data_element

  as select from ZI_MainBusinessObject
{
  key DocumentId,
      DocumentType,
      PostingDate
}
where PostingDate <= $session.system_date

My Questions:

  1. Is there a known limitation or missing @ui / @Consumption annotation required to bind a Value Help strictly to a PARAMETER (rather than a standard table field) in OData V4?
  2. Why is the OData V4 framework failing to resolve the context for the selected item despite the explicit localParameter binding?

Any insights or workarounds would be highly appreciated.

Thank you!

entry.title: Cancel the GRN document post UD Clearance in SAP Public cloud.
entry.content:
Hi Team,

User has performed the GRN and  also cleared the quality posting as well, now the post receiving the material into stock w.r.t Purchase order. they come to know that they have received the wrong product. Kindly suggest how to reverse the GRN Entry and again perform GRN w.r.t same Purchase order. So that the required action could be performed again. 

Below error is coming while performing the Cancellation :

You cannot cancel GR, since inspection lot is already partly posted

Message no. QA046

Diagnosis

You are attempting to reverse a goods receipt although quantity postings have already been made from the inspection lot. This means that stock for the goods receipt has already been transferred and, for this reason, a cancelation of the document is no longer possible.

 

Procedure

Ensure that the postings in QM (within the usage decision transactions) are carried out.
